page: UpCloud PREMIUM-64xCPU-384GB is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor. It features 64 physical cores and 64 vCPUs with a shared CPU allocation, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 384.0 GB of system memory, providing 6.0 GB of RAM per core, and a bundled 500 GB local SSD.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in several areas. The server achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU evaluations, as well as PassMark CPU, physics, and encryption tests. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is also in the top 10%, though large-block uncached read and write bandwidth drops to the top 25%, and the PassMark memory score sits in the average tier. In database and key-value store benchmarks, the system demonstrates high throughput, reaching 118.01 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and top-tier performance in PassMark database operations.

While the server delivers strong performance in single-threaded and multi-threaded compression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3, its LLM inference capabilities vary. It achieves top-tier speeds for small model prompt processing but drops to average performance for text generation. This hardware profile is suited for database hosting, static web serving, and parallelized data processing tasks that benefit from high core density and substantial memory capacity, though workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocations or massive uncached memory bandwidth may experience resource contention due to the shared CPU model.
description: The UpCloud PREMIUM-64xCPU-384GB is an x86_64 server featuring 64 AMD EPYC 9575F physical cores and vCPUs under a shared allocation model, running at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 500 GB local SSD. Benchmark results place this server in the top 10% for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance, as well as database operations, with Redis SET operations reaching 118.01 million operations per second. However, memory performance is mixed, with small-block cached reads in the top 10% while overall PassMark memory scores remain in the average tier. This server is suited for memory-intensive databases, static web serving, and parallel data processing workloads, though the shared CPU allocation represents a potential tradeoff for sustained, isolated compute requirements.
og_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-64xCPU-384GB is an x86_64 server with 64 AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB RAM, and a 500 GB SSD, delivering top-tier database and multi-core CPU performance under a shared allocation model.
meta_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-64xCPU-384GB is an x86_64 server featuring 64 AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB RAM, and a 500 GB SSD for database and compute workloads.
tagline: A high-density AMD EPYC server featuring sixty-four cores and three hundred eighty-four gigabytes of memory for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 AMD EPYC 9575F cores with shared CPU allocation
384 GB of system memory and a bundled 500 GB local SSD
Top-tier multi-core CPU and database benchmark performance
Redis throughput reaching 118.01 million operations per second
Suited for database hosting, web serving, and data processing
